what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon showing a three-part skyline with small filled window dots.
observations: Uses a 24x24 viewBox with no fixed width or height.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor on the root svg.
Uses three closed path shapes for staggered building forms rising from a shared baseline.
Adds five small circle elements as window dots with currentColor fill.
Uses round stroke caps and joins with a stroke width of 1.5.
standouts: The icon combines outline building silhouettes with filled dot windows.
All color is inherited through currentColor.
conventions: Follows common UI icon conventions: 24x24 viewBox, currentColor, rounded stroke styling, and no embedded color values.
Uses compact inline SVG markup without whitespace formatting.
Uses closed path outlines for building blocks rather than separate line segments.
concerns: The filled window circles also inherit the root stroke, which may make the rendered dots larger than the .5 radius alone suggests.
No title or accessible label is included in the SVG itself.
notes: A reusable currentColor skyline icon composed of three building outlines and five window dots.
questions: Should the window circles intentionally inherit the parent stroke as well as fill?
Is accessibility labeling expected to be supplied by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the filename: the SVG renders a skyline-style icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a small themeable skyline or city icon that adapts to surrounding text color.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor skyline symbol with staggered building geometry and dot-window detail for a fused icon set.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG icon depicting three staggered skyline buildings with five filled window dots.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a city, skyline, or urban setting as a reusable monochrome UI icon.
